title: Mercury Retrograde in Virgo
excerpt: When the messenger planet turns inward, the systems we rely on for clarity begin to speak in riddles.
content:
When the messenger planet turns inward, the systems we rely on for clarity begin to speak in riddles. Mercury retrograde in Virgo asks us to revisit not just our plans, but the very frameworks through which we organize experience.

Virgo is the sign of discernment, of the harvest, of knowing which grain to keep and which to let fall. Mercury here is already at home—precise, analytical, attentive to detail. When it stations retrograde in this earth sign, the invitation is to refine rather than to discard.

This is a moment for editing the inner monologue. For noticing where perfectionism has become paralysis. For finding the overlooked detail that, once corrected, allows everything else to flow.

title: Venus in Her Home Sign
excerpt: Venus in Libra is not about love as possession—it is love as art, as negotiation, as the mutual recognition of worth.
content:
Venus in Libra is not about love as possession—it is love as art, as negotiation, as the mutual recognition of worth.

When the planet of beauty and value returns to the sign she rules, there is a collective exhale. The world becomes briefly, genuinely interested in fairness. In aesthetic harmony. In the grace of two things in balance.

Watch your relationships this transit for what they reveal about your own unresolved agreements—the ones you made with yourself about what you deserve. Venus in Libra will surface them, gently, like a letter slid under a door.

title: The Saturn Return: A Rite of Passage
excerpt: Every 29 years, Saturn returns to the place it occupied at your birth. It does not come gently.
content:
Every 29 years, Saturn returns to the place it occupied at your birth. It does not come gently.

The Saturn return—occurring first around ages 27–30, then again at 56–60—is one of the most reliable rites of passage mapped by astrology. In the years leading up to it, life tends to accumulate: commitments made too quickly, structures that were never truly ours, identities borrowed from family or culture.

Saturn arrives and begins auditing. What is built on solid ground? What is constructed from avoidance? The return is not punishment—it is the planet of maturity insisting that we grow into ourselves.

Those who resist the changes Saturn demands often find them arriving anyway, through external circumstances that force the hand. Those who cooperate—who willingly dismantle what no longer serves—discover that the ringed planet is, ultimately, a builder.

title: Season of Depth
excerpt: Scorpio season does not ask what you want. It asks what you cannot live without.
content:
Scorpio season does not ask what you want. It asks what you cannot live without.

As the Sun moves through this fixed water sign each year, the collective attention turns underground. Toward what is hidden, what is shared, what has been inherited without our knowledge or consent.

This is the time when the veil between worlds grows thin—not only in the mythological sense, but in the psychological one. Emotions that have been successfully suppressed find new exits. Dreams grow stranger. The body's intelligence reasserts itself.

Scorpio season rewards honesty. Not the politeness of Libra season just past, but the harder, more surgical variety—the kind that locates the true source of discomfort and names it without flinching.

title: Where Jupiter Travels, Luck Follows
excerpt: Jupiter is the planet of increase. Whatever house it occupies in your chart becomes, for a year, the site of expansion.
content:
Jupiter is the planet of increase. Whatever house it occupies in your chart becomes, for a year, the site of expansion—sometimes welcome, sometimes excessive, always instructive.

We speak of Jupiter luck as though the planet drops gifts from the sky. In practice, Jupiter luck is more like a door that opens in a room you have been standing in for years. You were always capable of walking through it. Jupiter makes it visible.

The shadow of Jupiter is inflation: too much of even good things. Too much optimism, too much growth without integration, too much yes without discernment. The invitation of any Jupiter transit is to expand wisely—to receive abundance and know what to do with it.

title: Living by the Moon
excerpt: The Moon completes her cycle every 29.5 days. She does not rush. She does not slow down. She simply moves.
content:
The Moon completes her cycle every 29.5 days. She does not rush. She does not slow down. She simply moves.

Living by the Moon is not about superstition—it is about rhythm. The human body, like the ocean, is majority water. We are not separate from the pull of celestial cycles; we are subject to them, whether we acknowledge it or not.

New Moon: begin. Plant the seed of intention in the dark.
Waxing: build, accumulate, push forward.
Full Moon: illuminate. See what was hidden. Release what is complete.
Waning: integrate, rest, compost.

This is not metaphor. This is a map. The Moon offers us a template for cyclical living in a culture that only values the waxing phase.
